creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Martin Birgmeier from comment #4)

Did you set WITH_WX_VER in /etc/make.conf as indicated in /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.wx.mk?  Try setting

    WITH_WX_VER=3.2

to force version 3.2.

The ports infrastructure is not designed to configure ports based on what is already installed on the system.  Instead, it chooses dependencies based on what you configured.  Interestingly enough, bsd.wx.mk does have some sort of logic to detect existing wx installtions, but I don't really get how it works.  The current version only checks for 3.0 and 3.2 anyway.

As this doesn't seem to be specific to hugin in any way, I've assigned the bug to desktop@, which is the group taking care of bsd.wx.mk.  Maybe someone there has something to say about this.
